data.content: Your code already uses `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`, which is the standard abstraction. I'll generate a project-specific wrapper interface and implementation that provides a simplified API on top of it, giving you a facade you own with the most common operations.
data.reasoningText:
The user has a simple codebase using `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`. They want me to generate a file system wrapper. 

But wait — they're already using `IFileSystem` from `System.IO.Abstractions`, which IS the file system abstraction/wrapper. What could they mean by "generate a file system wrapper"?

I think they might want me to create a concrete implementation/wrapper class that wraps the real file system and implements `IFileSystem`, or more likely they want a custom wrapper/facade around `IFileSystem` that provides a simplified interface for their specific use cases.

Actually, looking at this more carefully — `System.IO.Abstractions` already provides `FileSystem` as the concrete implementation of `IFileSystem`. 

So they might be looking for a custom facade that exposes only the file operations they actually need, rather than the full `IFileSystem` surface. Or possibly they want a wrapper they can register in their dependency injection container. Let me think about what would be most useful for their scenario. That makes sense — I'll create a simple wrapper interface and implementation that sits on top of `IFileSystem`, giving them a project-specific abstraction layer they can control. I'll focus on the most commonly used file operations to keep it practical.

Creating a wrapper service...

toolCalls.name: create
create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-14d6a0c979cf4ed0958783d66c05c107/Services/FileServiceWrapper.cs b/tmp/sv-14d6a0c979cf4ed0958783d66c05c107/Services/FileServiceWrapper.cs
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-14d6a0c979cf4ed0958783d66c05c107/Services/FileServiceWrapper.cs
@@ -1,0 +1,90 @@
+using System.IO.Abstractions;
+
+namespace MyApp.Services;
+
+/// <summary>
+/// Concrete wrapper that delegates every call to an
+/// <see cref="IFileSystem"/> instance provided by System.IO.Abstractions.
+/// Register as a singleton (or scoped) in your DI container.
+/// </summary>
+public class FileServiceWrapper(IFileSystem fileSystem) : IFileServiceWrapper
+{
+    // ── File operations ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public string ReadAllText(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllText(path);
+
+    public byte[] ReadAllBytes(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllBytes(path);
+
+    public string[] ReadAllLines(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.ReadAllLines(path);
+
+    public void WriteAllText(string path, string contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllText(path, contents);
+
+    public void WriteAllBytes(string path, byte[] bytes) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllBytes(path, bytes);
+
+    public void WriteAllLines(string path, IEnumerable<string> contents) =>
+        fileSystem.File.WriteAllLines(path, contents);
+
+    public Stream OpenRead(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.OpenRead(path);
+
+    public Stream Create(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Create(path);
+
+    public bool FileExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Exists(path);
+
+    public void DeleteFile(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Delete(path);
+
+    public void CopyFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Copy(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    public void MoveFile(string sourcePath, string destPath, bool overwrite = false) =>
+        fileSystem.File.Move(sourcePath, destPath, overwrite);
+
+    // ── Directory operations ────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public bool DirectoryExists(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Exists(path);
+
+    public void CreateDirectory(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.CreateDirectory(path);
+
+    public void DeleteDirectory(string path, bool recursive = false) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.Delete(path, recursive);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> GetFiles(
+        string path,
+        string searchPattern = "*",
+        SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetFiles(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    public IEnumerable<string> GetDirectories(
+        string path,
+        string searchPattern = "*",
+        SearchOption searchOption = SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly) =>
+        fileSystem.Directory.GetDirectories(path, searchPattern, searchOption);
+
+    // ── Path operations ─────────────────────────────────────────────
+
+    public string Combine(params string[] paths) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.Combine(paths);
+
+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;
+
+    public string GetFileName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFileName(path);
+
+    public string GetFullPath(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetFullPath(path);
+
+    public string GetTempPath() =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetTempPath();
+}
+

+

+    public string GetDirectoryName(string path) =>
+        fileSystem.Path.GetDirectoryName(path)!;

data.content:
Created two files in `Services/`:

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `IFileServiceWrapper.cs` | Interface you own with file, directory, and path operations |
| `FileServiceWrapper.cs` | Delegates to `IFileSystem` via primary constructor |

**Covers:** `ReadAllText/Bytes/Lines`, `WriteAllText/Bytes/Lines`, `OpenRead`, `Create`, `FileExists`, `Delete`, `Copy`, `Move`, `DirectoryExists`, `CreateDirectory`, `DeleteDirectory`, `GetFiles`, `GetDirectories`, and path helpers (`Combine`, `GetDirectoryName`, `GetFileName`, `GetFullPath`, `GetTempPath`).

**DI registration:**
```csharp
services.AddSingleton<IFileServiceWrapper, FileServiceWrapper>();
```

Since your `DataService` already injects `IFileSystem`, you can swap it to use `IFileServiceWrapper` instead to decouple from the large `System.IO.Abstractions` surface. The wrapper is fully testable — just mock `IFileServiceWrapper` directly or pass a `MockFileSystem` into `FileServiceWrapper`.
